title: Device and method for enhancing contact pressure on electrodes and reducing impedance
abstract: A Self-Pressing Adhesive Gel Electrode Pad (SPAGEP) can press an electrode against the skin of a patient to reduce impedance for a countershock. The SPAGEP can have an Electrode Contact Force System (ECFS) that can include a pusher to provide force to the electrode, an outer shell to provide support to the pusher, thereby allowing the pusher to press against the electrode, and a fastener to fasten the outer shell to the patient so that the outer shell can support the pusher when the pusher is providing force to the electrode.
claims: Claims 1. A Self-Pressing Adhesive Gel Electrode Pad (SPAGEP) comprising: an electrode adapted for applying a countershock to a patent; an Electrode Contact Force System (ECFS) for applying contact force to the electrode, the electrode contact force system comprising: a pusher adapted to press the electrode against the skin of the patient; an outer shell adapted to provide a counterforce for the pusher, so that the pusher can press the electrode against the skin of the patient; and a fastener to fasten the ECFS to the patient over the electrode so that the outer shell can provide a counterforce to the pusher, thereby enabling the pusher to press the electrode against the skin of the patient without the need for external forces. 2. The SPAGEP of claim 1, wherein the fastener is an adhesive arranged around an outer rim of a patient facing surface of the ECFS. 3. The SPAGEP of claim 1, further comprising a secondary buttress adapted to provide additional fastening strength securing the ECFS to the patient. 4. The SPAGEP of claim 1, wherein the electrode further comprises a conductive gel coating a patient-facing side of the electrode, and wherein the ECFS further comprises a thermal heater, the thermal heater adapted to warm the gel and warm the skin of the patient. 5. The SPAGEP of claim 1, wherein the pusher comprises a bladder that can be inflated to provide a pushing force to press on the electrode. 6. The SPAGEP of claim 1, wherein the pusher comprises a bladder containing a first fluid and capsule within the bladder, the capsule containing a second fluid, wherein rupturing of the capsule allows the fluids to mix and react, thereby enlarging the bladder. 7. The SPAGEP of claim 6, wherein the first fluid and the second fluid create an exothermic reaction when mixed, the exothermic reaction providing heat to warm a conductive gel on a patient facing surface of the electrode. 8. The SPAGEP of claim 1, further comprising a plurality of pushers adapted to press on a plurality of electrodes. 9. An Electrode Contact Force System (ECFS) for applying contact force to an electrode, the electrode contact force system comprising: a pusher adapted to press an electrode against the skin of the patient; an outer shell adapted to provide a counterforce for the pusher, so that the pusher can press the electrode against the skin of the patient; and a fastener to fasten the ECFS to the patient over the electrode so that the outer shell can provide a counterforce to the pusher, thereby enabling the pusher to press the electrode against the skin of the patient. 10. The ECFS of claim 9, wherein the fastener is an adhesive arranged around an outer rim of a patient facing surface of the ECFS. 11. The ECFS of claim 9, wherein the fastener is a belt adapted to be wrapped around the patient. 12. The ECFS of claim 9, further comprising a secondary buttress adapted to provide additional fastening strength securing the ECFS to the patient. 13. The ECFS of claim 9, wherein the pusher comprises a bladder that can be inflated to provide a pushing force to press on the electrode. 14. The ECFS of claim 9, wherein the pusher comprises a bladder containing a first chemical and capsule within the bladder, the capsule containing a second chemical, wherein rupturing of the capsule allows the chemicals to mix and react, thereby enlarging the bladder. 15. The ECFS of claim 9, further comprising a thermal heater for warming the gel and adapted to warm the skin of the patient. 16. The ECFS of claim 9, wherein the pusher comprises a mechanical actuator adapted to provide contact force pressing the electrode against the skin of the patient. 17. A method of applying a countershock comprising: applying an electrode to a patient; applying an Electrode Contact Force System (ECFS) over the electrode, the electrode contact force system including a pusher adapted to press an electrode against the skin of the patient, an outer shell adapted to provide a counterforce for the pusher so that the pusher can press the electrode against the skin of the patient, and a fastener to fasten the ECFS to the patient over the electrode so that the outer shell can provide a counterforce to the pusher; triggering the pusher to press the electrode against the skin of the patient, thereby reducing the impedance of current flow though the patient; and providing the patient with a countershock. 18. The method of applying a countershock of claim 17, further comprising measuring the impedance of current through the patient before providing the electroshock, and adjusting the force from the pusher to the electrode to minimize impedance. 19. The method of applying a countershock of claim 17, wherein applying an electrode to a patient is a separate step of applying a first component, and applying an ECFS is a separate step of applying a second component.
